title: Project Governance
description: Quiz covering project governance, governance roles, accountability, decision-making, strategic oversight and project performance.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of project governance?
options: To provide strategic direction, oversight and accountability throughout the project
To create the Work Breakdown Structure
To develop the project schedule
To replace the project manager
questions.explanation: Project governance provides the framework for directing, monitoring and controlling projects while ensuring they support organizational objectives.

questions.question: Project governance mainly focuses on:
options: Strategic oversight and major decision-making
Completing daily project tasks
Writing technical specifications
Managing employee salaries
questions.explanation: Governance focuses on strategic direction, accountability and oversight rather than daily project execution.

questions.question: Who is primarily responsible for the day-to-day management of a project?
options: The Project Manager
The Steering Committee
The Project Sponsor
The Board of Directors
questions.explanation: The Project Manager is responsible for planning, executing and controlling the project's daily activities.

questions.question: Which governance role is typically responsible for providing strategic direction and securing project funding?
options: Project Sponsor
Project Engineer
Supplier
Customer
questions.explanation: The Project Sponsor provides strategic leadership, secures funding and is ultimately accountable for project success.

questions.question: Which group commonly reviews major project decisions such as large budget increases or significant scope changes?
options: The Steering Committee
The Project Team
The Procurement Department
The Quality Inspector
questions.explanation: The Steering Committee provides oversight and reviews major strategic decisions.

questions.question: How does project governance differ from project management?
options: Governance provides oversight, while project management focuses on daily execution
Governance creates the project schedule, while project management creates contracts
There is no practical difference
Governance only exists after project completion
questions.explanation: Project management executes the project, while governance ensures it remains aligned with organizational objectives.

questions.question: Why is clear decision-making authority important in project governance?
options: It prevents confusion about who is responsible for making different decisions
It eliminates all project risks
It guarantees every project finishes early
It reduces the need for communication
questions.explanation: Clearly defined authority helps projects avoid delays caused by uncertainty and conflicting decisions.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a governance responsibility?
options: Approving a major increase in the project budget
Updating the daily task list
Assigning tomorrow's work schedule
Inspecting completed concrete work
questions.explanation: Strategic financial decisions are normally handled by the governance structure rather than the project manager.

questions.question: A project manager wants to increase the project budget by 20%. What should normally happen?
options: The request should be escalated to the appropriate governance body for approval
The project manager should approve it independently
The supplier should decide
No approval is required
questions.explanation: Major financial decisions usually require approval from the Project Sponsor or Steering Committee.

questions.question: Why do organizations establish project governance structures?
options: To ensure projects remain aligned with business strategy while maintaining accountability
To reduce the number of stakeholders
To eliminate project management
To remove all project risks
questions.explanation: Project governance helps organizations ensure projects deliver value while supporting strategic objectives.

questions.question: A project manager discovers that a design change will increase project costs by 15%. What should normally happen next?
options: Escalate the issue to the appropriate governance body for review and approval
Approve the additional cost without informing anyone
Ignore the cost increase until project completion
Ask the supplier to decide
questions.explanation: Significant cost changes are strategic decisions that normally require approval from the project's governance structure.

questions.question: Which situation should most likely be escalated to the Steering Committee?
options: A major scope change affecting the project's strategic objectives
A team member requesting a vacation day
A spelling mistake in a project report
A daily stand-up meeting taking five minutes longer than planned
questions.explanation: Strategic issues with major business impact should be reviewed by the Steering Committee.

questions.question: Why should routine operational decisions normally remain with the Project Manager?
options: To allow faster decision-making and avoid unnecessary escalation
Because governance bodies are not allowed to make decisions
Because project sponsors cannot attend meetings
Because operational decisions have no impact on the project
questions.explanation: Delegating operational decisions to the Project Manager improves efficiency while allowing governance to focus on strategic matters.

questions.question: Which of the following best demonstrates accountability in project governance?
options: Clearly defining who is responsible for making specific project decisions
Allowing everyone to approve major changes
Avoiding written responsibilities
Changing decision-makers every month
questions.explanation: Accountability requires clear ownership of responsibilities and decision-making authority.

questions.question: A project reports that it is likely to exceed its approved budget by 10%. What is the primary purpose of escalating this information?
options: To allow governance bodies to evaluate strategic options before the situation worsens
To remove the Project Manager
To automatically cancel the project
To avoid updating project reports
questions.explanation: Governance bodies provide strategic oversight and decide how significant project issues should be addressed.

questions.question: Why do governance bodies regularly review project performance reports?
options: To monitor whether the project continues to meet its strategic objectives
To manage employees' daily schedules
To replace the project manager
To reduce the number of stakeholders
questions.explanation: Performance reporting allows governance bodies to monitor project health and make informed strategic decisions.

questions.question: Which Key Performance Indicator (KPI) would be most useful for a governance board reviewing project performance?
options: Budget status, schedule performance and major project risks
The project team's lunch schedule
The number of emails sent each day
The color of the project documentation
questions.explanation: Governance focuses on high-level indicators that reflect project performance and organizational value.

questions.question: What is the greatest risk of having unclear governance roles?
options: Important decisions may be delayed or made by the wrong people
The project schedule automatically becomes shorter
Project costs always decrease
Stakeholders become unnecessary
questions.explanation: Unclear governance structures often result in confusion, duplicated effort and slow decision-making.

questions.question: A governance board frequently becomes involved in approving small day-to-day operational decisions. What is the most likely consequence?
options: Project progress slows because governance is micromanaging daily operations
The project automatically becomes more efficient
The Project Manager becomes unnecessary
Project risks disappear
questions.explanation: Governance should focus on strategic oversight rather than routine operational management.

questions.question: Which statement best describes effective project governance?
options: It provides strategic oversight while allowing the Project Manager sufficient authority to manage daily work
It requires senior management to approve every task
It replaces project management completely
It focuses only on project finances
questions.explanation: Successful governance balances executive oversight with operational autonomy, allowing the Project Manager to efficiently lead the project.

questions.question: A project has experienced several months of delays because no one is certain who has authority to approve major changes. Which governance weakness is most likely responsible?
options: Unclear decision-making authority
Too many project milestones
A detailed Work Breakdown Structure
Frequent team meetings
questions.explanation: An effective governance structure clearly defines who has authority to make different types of project decisions.

questions.question: A project sponsor wants to approve every small operational decision made by the project team. What is the most likely consequence?
options: Project progress slows because decision-making becomes centralized unnecessarily
The project automatically becomes more successful
The need for a Project Manager disappears
Project risks are eliminated
questions.explanation: Governance should provide oversight, not micromanage daily project activities.

questions.question: Which project issue is most appropriate for escalation to the governance board?
options: A significant legal risk that could affect the organization's reputation
A minor formatting error in a weekly report
A team member requesting new office equipment
The location of the next project meeting
questions.explanation: Strategic risks with significant organizational impact should be escalated to the governance body.

questions.question: Why is accountability considered a fundamental principle of project governance?
options: Because every important decision should have a clearly responsible owner
Because accountability eliminates all project risks
Because it allows every stakeholder to make decisions
Because it replaces project planning
questions.explanation: Clear accountability ensures that responsibilities are understood and that decisions can be made efficiently.

questions.question: A governance board receives reports showing increasing schedule delays, rising costs and growing project risks. What should the board do?
options: Evaluate strategic options and provide direction to keep the project aligned with organizational objectives
Ignore the reports until project completion
Replace the entire project team immediately
Stop all project activities without further analysis
questions.explanation: Governance exists to review significant project issues and make strategic decisions that support project success.

questions.question: A Project Manager decides to approve a major scope increase without consulting the Project Sponsor, even though governance procedures require sponsor approval. What governance principle has been violated?
options: Decision-making authority
Quality management
Procurement planning
Resource leveling
questions.explanation: Governance structures define which decisions can be made by the Project Manager and which require higher-level approval.

questions.question: Which statement best describes the relationship between project governance and organizational strategy?
options: Project governance ensures that project decisions continue to support the organization's long-term objectives
Project governance focuses only on technical project details
Project governance exists independently of business strategy
Project governance only becomes important after project completion
questions.explanation: Governance helps ensure that projects continue delivering strategic value throughout their lifecycle.

questions.question: An organization rarely reviews project performance after approval. What is the greatest governance risk?
options: Serious problems may remain unnoticed until they become difficult and expensive to correct
Projects will automatically finish ahead of schedule
Project Managers gain too much technical knowledge
Stakeholder communication becomes unnecessary
questions.explanation: Regular governance reviews help identify issues early, allowing corrective actions before problems escalate.

questions.question: Which governance approach is most likely to contribute to successful project delivery?
options: Clear roles, defined decision authority, regular performance reviews and appropriate escalation of major issues
Approving every operational decision at executive level
Allowing anyone to authorize budget changes
Avoiding governance meetings to reduce administration
questions.explanation: Successful governance combines accountability, structured oversight and efficient decision-making.

questions.question: What is the ultimate objective of project governance?
options: To ensure projects deliver value by providing strategic oversight, accountability and informed decision-making while allowing effective day-to-day project management
To replace the Project Manager in all decisions
To approve every project activity personally
To eliminate the need for stakeholders
questions.explanation: Project governance exists to maximize project success through strategic oversight, accountability and alignment with organizational objectives—not by managing daily operations.
